section_title: 10 DETAILS OF THE UNDERLYING SCENARIOS USED TO CALCULATE THE IMPACTS

scenarios.title: A1 - raw material supply
scenarios.description: This module takes into account the extraction and processing of all raw materials and energy which occur upstream to the studied manufacturing process.

scenarios.title: A2 – transport to the manufacturer
scenarios.description: The raw materials are transported to the manufacturing site.

scenarios.title: A3 - manufacturing
scenarios.description: This module considers the production process, including waste generated during production, as well as treatment of waste coming from raw materials packaging.

scenarios.title: A4 – transport to the building site
scenarios.description:
Fuel type and consumption of vehicle or vehicle type used for transport: Truck 16-32 ton, EURO6
Distance: 100 km
Capacity utilization (including empty returns): 50%
Bulk density of transported products: -
Volume capacity utilization factor: 1

The default B-EPD PCR scenario of 100 km has been considered.

scenarios.title: A5 – installation in the building
scenarios.description:
At the construction site, packaging materials become waste. The impact of packaging's EOL treatment has been included in this module (until end-of-waste point). All other processes and materials are out of scope of this study,

Packaging waste type | Quantity, kg | Description
Wood | 1.69E-02, 7.38E-02, 1.78E-01 | RECYCLED, INCINERATED, LANDFILLED
Cardboard | 2.03E-01, 1.92E-02, 1.49E-02 | RECYCLED, INCINERATED, LANDFILLED
Plastic | 2.36E-01, 5.03E-01, 2.86E-01 | RECYCLED, INCINERATED, LANDFILLED

scenarios.title: C: End of life
scenarios.description:
The following scenarios have been used for different materials to model end-of-life stage:

Sorting | Landfill | Incineration | Recycling
Aluminium: 100% | 4% | - | 96%
Rubbers: - | 1% | 99% | -
Plastic parts: - | 10% | 90% | -
Other metal parts: - | 5% | - | 95%
Coating, sealants, glues: - | - | 100% | -

C1: No input is required to dismantle the window, as it is manual process.
C2: Default transport scenarios for Belgium have been considered for transport of waste.
C3: Only aluminium profiles & other metals are recycled at the end-of-life of the window.
C4: All plastic parts are either incinerated or landfilled at the end-of-life. Coatings, sealants, glues are incinerated with the low efficiency.

Module C2 – Transport to waste processing:
Type of vehicle (truck/boat/etc.): Truck 16-32 t, EURO5
Fuel consumption (litres/km): Default from ecoinvent 3.9.1
Distance (km): Sorting: 30 km, Landfill: 50 km, Incineration: 150 km, Recycling: 200 km
Capacity utilization (%): 50%
Density of products (kg/m3): -

End-of-life modules – C3 and C4:
Parameter | Value (kg)
Wastes collected separately: 14.4
Wastes collected as mixed construction waste: 4.23
Waste for re-use: 0
Waste for recycling: 14.4
Waste for energy recovery: 0
Waste for final disposal: 4.23

scenarios.title: D – Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries
scenarios.description:
The following waste streams are considered in module D: aluminium, other metal parts, rubbers, coatings, sealants as well as the packaging of the window systems sent to treatment in A5 stage.

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE LOADS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES:
Sorting & pressing of 12.6 kg of aluminium for recycling
Sorting & pressing of 1.25 kg of metals for recycling
Sorting & recycling of 0.23 kg of plastic waste (packaging)
Sorting & shredding of 0.017 kg of wood waste (packaging)
Sorting of 0.203 kg of paper waste (packaging)

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE BENEFITS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES:
Avoided production of 12.6 kg primary aluminium
Avoided production of 1.25 kg primary brass
Avoided production of 0.23 kg primary PE (packaging)
Avoided use of 0.017 kg of wood (packaging)
